message:
feat(extensions): structured concurrency with ExtensionRegion RAII guard

Introduce ExtensionRegion, an RAII wrapper around ExtensionManager that
guarantees the JS extension runtime thread is shut down when the session
region exits.  This closes the lifecycle gap where orphaned runtime
threads could outlive their parent AgentSession.

Key changes:

- ExtensionRegion: new-type with configurable cleanup budget (default 5s),
  AtomicBool shutdown tracking, and best-effort Drop that relies on Weak
  reference expiry for threads not explicitly shut down
- JsExtensionRuntimeHandle::shutdown(): sends Shutdown command then awaits
  an exit_signal oneshot from the runtime thread, bounded by budget
- ExtensionManager gains Budget integration (with_budget, set_budget,
  extension_cx) for structured concurrency via asupersync::Budget
- ExtensionManager::shutdown(): delegates to JsExtensionRuntimeHandle and
  clears the handle so subsequent calls are no-ops
- JsRuntimeHost: manager field migrated from ExtensionManager to
  Arc::downgrade (Weak<Mutex<ExtensionManagerInner>>), with manager()
  accessor returning Option<ExtensionManager> — hostcall dispatchers now
  gracefully deny with SHUTDOWN error code when the manager is dropped
- AgentSession::extensions changed from Option<ExtensionManager> to
  Option<ExtensionRegion>; callers access via region.manager()
- RPC layer: create_provider and extension cloning updated to go through
  the ExtensionRegion accessor
- All test JsRuntimeHost constructions updated to use Arc::downgrade

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
